---
title: Identifying Fraud
subtitle: An analysis of fraud detection using statistical tools and techniques
author: Muireann Ryle
---

This project examined an artificial dataset of 500 transactions where 246 were fraudulent, and 254 were not.
There were 23 variables, 11 of which were categorical. I used a mixture of Python and PowerBI to perform
Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) and build a predictor for fraud amount.

The first step taken consisted of preparing the data for EDA. This included **data normalisation**, 
calculating the **interquartile range (IQR)**, identifying **outliers**, and creating **z-scores**.
```{.python} 
# Compute z-scores
z_scores = (fraud_df[cols] - fraud_df[cols].mean()) / fraud_df[cols].std()

# Add to original DataFrame
fraud_df_z = fraud_df.copy()
fraud_df_z[[f'{col}_z' for col in cols]] = z_scores

#IQR
Q1 = fraud_df['transaction_amount'].quantile(0.25)
Q3 = fraud_df['transaction_amount'].quantile(0.75)
IQR = Q3 - Q1

# Define bounds
lower_bound = Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
upper_bound = Q3 + 1.5 * IQR

# Flag outliers
outliers_iqr = fraud_df[(fraud_df['transaction_amount'] < lower_bound) | (fraud_df['transaction_amount'] > upper_bound)]
print(outliers_iqr[['transaction_amount', 'is_fraud']])
```
